summary: Implemented TimerService with timestamp-based countdown, pause/resume, skip, adjust, persistState/recoverState for foreground and background reliability. All state transitions tested with 34 unit tests at 96.89% coverage.
keyDecisions: Timestamp-based calculation (Date.now()) instead of relative countdown for reliability across app lifecycle
TimerPhase internal state machine: idle -> running -> paused -> idle for clear state transitions
On pause, store pausedAtRemaining; on resume, recalculate startedAt so timestamp math stays consistent
persistState uses JSON serialization with BigInt as string for user_settings storage under key 'active_timer_state'
recoverState computes elapsed time from persisted startedAt, fires onComplete if timer already expired (rest time has passed)
adjust() recalculates startedAt and totalDuration to maintain timestamp-based countdown integrity

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: start(durationSeconds) begins countdown with startedAt = Date.now()
Timer based on timestamp calculation: remaining = totalDuration - (now - startedAt)/1000
adjust(deltaSeconds) extends or shortens the remaining time
persistState() saves timer state (startedAt, totalDuration, exerciseBizKey) to user_settings
recoverState() restores timer from persisted state, computes elapsed time
Background: expo-background-fetch keeps timer alive when app backgrounded
Notification: expo-notifications schedules local notification on timer completion
Force-close recovery: on app reopen, recoverState() computes from startedAt, shows alert if elapsed > totalDuration
Unit tests for timer state transitions and recovery logic

notes: Background fetch and notification scheduling are integration-layer concerns that consume this service's onComplete callback. The service provides the core timestamp-based timer logic; expo-background-fetch and expo-notifications integration will be wired in the hook/store layer. TimerService interface updated to accept optional exerciseBizKey in start().
